name: deslopify
description: A universal MCP server that acts as a code quality gate for AI assistants

tools.name: get_guidance
tools.description: Get language-specific guidance, idioms, and pitfalls before writing code

tools.name: review_code
tools.description: Review generated code for quality issues, returns score and fix suggestions

tools.name: validate_approach
tools.description: Validate whether a proposed bug fix targets root cause or just patches a symptom

tools.name: suggest_tests
tools.description: Generate structured test cases with framework-specific skeletons

tools.name: check_dependencies
tools.description: Evaluate whether proposed dependencies are appropriate or overkill

tools.name: check_api_exists
tools.description: Verify whether an API exists in the target language and version

tools.name: review_codebase
tools.description: Review an entire codebase for quality issues, returns per-file scores, overall score, worst files, and most common issues

prompts.name: pre_generation
prompts.description: Consult Deslopify before generating any code for language-specific guidance
prompts.text: Before writing any code for this task, call the Deslopify get_guidance tool with the language and task. Follow the returned guidance strictly — use recommended idioms, make the solution generic, implement error handling, and ensure testability. Do not proceed until you have reviewed the guidance.

prompts.name: post_generation
prompts.description: Submit generated code to Deslopify for review before presenting to user
prompts.text: Before presenting this code to the user, call the Deslopify review_code tool with language ${arguments.language} and code ${arguments.code}. If the verdict is needs_improvement or fail, address all errors, regenerate the code, and review again. Do not present code until the verdict is pass or all errors are resolved. Include suggested test cases.

prompts.name: bug_fix_validation
prompts.description: Validate a proposed bug fix targets root cause not just the symptom
prompts.text: Before implementing a fix, call the Deslopify validate_approach tool with language ${arguments.language}, problem_description ${arguments.problem}, and proposed_approach ${arguments.approach}. If the assessment is symptom_fix, do NOT proceed. Follow the recommendation to trace the root cause, ask clarifying questions, and reformulate. Only implement when assessment is root_cause_fix or likely_root_cause.

prompts.name: full_workflow
prompts.description: Complete Deslopify workflow: guidance, validation, generation, review, tests
prompts.text: Follow the complete Deslopify workflow for ${arguments.task} in ${arguments.language}: 1) Call get_guidance for language idioms. 2) If fixing a bug, call validate_approach to confirm root cause. 3) Generate code following guidance. 4) Call review_code and iterate until pass. 5) Call suggest_tests and include test cases. 6) If adding packages, call check_dependencies. Present final code, review results, and tests together.
